transcript_object.content: Hi, this is MindVault AI Studio. I'm calling about the AI Assessment you booked. Before we dive in, tell me about your business—what industry are you in, and what do you specialize in?
Hey yeah, I'm Mike. I run a roofing company here in Phoenix—Desert Peak Roofing. We do mostly residential, some commercial. Been at it about 8 years now.
Great, thanks Mike. How big is your team?
I've got 12 guys on the crew, plus my wife handles the office. She's overwhelmed honestly, trying to keep up with all the calls and scheduling.
I hear that a lot. Walk me through your typical day—what time do you start, and what eats up most of your time?

I'm usually out on jobs by 7 AM. But man, I spend at least 3-4 hours every evening doing admin stuff. Quotes, following up on estimates, trying to keep track of everything. It's burning me out. I feel like I'm working two jobs—the roofing and then this second shift of paperwork.
That's a heavy load. If you picture a perfect week, what would need to change?
Honestly? I just want my evenings back. I'd love to spend time with my kids instead of drowning in spreadsheets. If I could cut that admin time in half, it'd be life-changing.
What would you say is the single biggest frustration in your business right now?

The missed calls kill me. I get home from a job and have 15 voicemails. By the time I call back, they've already hired someone else. I probably lose 30% of leads just from not answering fast enough. It's brutal during storm season—volume goes crazy.
Let's talk about your current tools. What are you using for email, CRM, scheduling?

Gmail for email. We tried a CRM—Zoho I think—but it was too complicated. My wife uses a paper calendar for scheduling. Everything else is spreadsheets. It's a mess honestly. We tried to go digital but it felt like more work than it saved.
Do you have any automation running between your tools?
Nope. Nothing automated. Everything's manual. My wife copies info from emails into the calendar, then into a spreadsheet for tracking. It's duplicate work everywhere.
Last question—if we could implement 2-3 AI tools that actually worked, what would success look like in 6 months?

I'd have evenings back. We'd be capturing way more leads—maybe close that 30% gap. And my wife wouldn't be stressed out juggling everything. If it actually worked, I'd probably hire more crew and grow this thing. Right now I'm capped because I can't handle more admin.

analysis.summary: Discovery call completed. Customer runs 8-year-old roofing company with 12 employees. Major pain points: 30% lead loss from slow response, 3-4 hours daily admin burden, no CRM, manual processes throughout.
